title: From “Later Life” by Christina Rossetti — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
VI
We lack, yet cannot fix upon the lack:
Not this, nor that; yet somewhat, certainly.
We see the things we do not yearn to see
Around us: and what see we glancing back?
Lost hopes that leave our hearts upon the rack,
Hopes that were never ours yet seem’d to be,
For which we steer’d on life’s salt stormy sea
Braving the sunstroke and the frozen pack.
If thus to look behind is all in vain,
And all in vain to look to left or right,
Why face we not our future once again,
meaning: "From “Later Life”" by Christina Rossetti is a sad and love and nature and inspirational english poem consisting of 31 lines. This English poem by Christina Rossetti demonstrates the timeless power of verse to capture complex human emotions. Beginning with "VI We lack, yet cannot fix upon the lack:...", this piece explores themes of sad and love and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional resonance. The work invites contemplation on the deeper currents of life, love, and the human condition. Christina Rossetti's celebrated body of poetry continues to inspire readers across generations and cultures, and this particular work stands as a powerful example of their artistic vision.
